query: 2026 Venezuela earthquakes June 24 confirmed death toll latest figures, trajectory of confirmed deaths reported by authorities and Wikipedia, number of missing persons and rescue operation status
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the June 24, 2026 Venezuela earthquakes:

**Death Toll Trajectory (official/authorities):** The confirmed toll rose steadily as bodies were recovered — from ~235 on day one (June 24) to **3,889 as of July 9, 2026** (government/National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez). Intermediate milestones: 1,719 (Jun 29), 2,595 (Jul 1), 3,535 (Jul 6-7).

**Wikipedia vs. authorities discrepancy:** Wikipedia's article states "more than 4,300 killed, 16,700 injured, tens of thousands missing" — a death figure **higher** than the official confirmed toll of 3,889. USGS PAGER predicted the toll could exceed 10,000, and CNN noted the official count seemed "remarkably low" relative to the destruction.

**Missing persons:** Consistently "tens of thousands"; the IRC estimated **~50,000 remain missing** under rubble. No precise official government figure was confirmed.

**Rescue status:** Peaked at 2,000+ workers from 27 countries; the 72-hour window passed ~June 27-28 with live rescues continuing beyond it. By July 9, operations had shifted to debris removal, body recovery, and displaced-family aid in La Guaira, with 6,462 survivors rescued alive cumulatively. Injured ~16,740; displaced 17,907 across 87 shelters.

The death toll trajectory was **still rising** as of the latest data (July 9), so figures are not final. Full structured findings, timeline, and sources have been submitted.

key_facts: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on 24 June 2026 at 18:04 VET: a Mw 7.2 foreshock followed 39 seconds later by a Mw 7.5 mainshock, epicenters in Veroes Municipality, Yaracuy.
Latest official confirmed death toll: 3,889 (government/National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez, July 9 2026); WMNF cited 3,899 via El Nacional same day.
Wikipedia's article states 'more than 4,300 killed, 16,700 injured, tens of thousands missing' — a death figure higher than the official confirmed toll of 3,889.
USGS PAGER system predicted the death toll could potentially exceed 10,000.
Displaced/unhoused: 17,907 people housed across 87 temporary shelters (July 9); earlier ~12,000-12,721.
Missing persons: consistently described as 'tens of thousands'; IRC estimated ~50,000 remain missing; no precise official figure confirmed.
Rescue peaked at 2,000+ workers from 27 countries, 40+ teams, 160+ search dogs (~June 29); 72-hour window passed ~June 27-28 with live rescues continuing.
6,462 survivors rescued alive cumulatively (July 9); 774 buildings collapsed, ~2,500 structures damaged; national water system failed in seven states.
La Guaira state saw ~80% of buildings collapse; deadliest natural disaster in Venezuela in over a century and strongest quake since 1900.
PAHO warned of disease-outbreak risk; UN appealed for ~$300M and IOM for $98M in humanitarian aid.
